---
title: "STAT 5703 - HW 4"
format: html
---

```{r}
# =====================================================================
# Setup
# Load the required library in a single command
# Load the data and assign it a sensible variable name
# =====================================================================
timber_data <- trees
timber_data
```

```{r}
# =====================================================================
# Part A
# Obtain and print the scatterplot matrix of all variables
# =====================================================================
pairs(timber_data)
```

```{r}
# =====================================================================
# Part B
# Check the normal distribution of the response variable Volume
# Generate a Q-Q plot and run a Shapiro-Wilk hypothesis test
# =====================================================================
qqnorm(timber_data$Volume)
qqline(timber_data$Volume)

shapiro.test(timber_data$Volume)
```

```{r}
# =====================================================================
# Part C
# Fit regular multiple regression model on log-transformed predictors
# Output the summary to check predictor significance
# =====================================================================
model_lm <- lm(Volume ~ log(Girth) + log(Height), data = timber_data)
summary(model_lm)
```

```{r}
# =====================================================================
# Part D
# Fit Gamma GLM with log link predicting Volume with intercept only
# Report the residual deviance
# =====================================================================
model_gamma_null <- glm(Volume ~ 1, family = Gamma(link = "log"), data = timber_data)
summary(model_gamma_null)$deviance
```

```{r}
# =====================================================================
# Part E
# Fit Gamma GLM with log link predicting Volume by log(Girth)
# Report the residual deviance
# =====================================================================
model_gamma_girth <- glm(Volume ~ log(Girth), family = Gamma(link = "log"), data = timber_data)
summary(model_gamma_girth)$deviance
```

```{r}
# =====================================================================
# Part F
# Fit Gamma GLM with log link predicting Volume by log(Girth) and log(Height)
# Report the residual deviance
# =====================================================================
model_gamma_full <- glm(Volume ~ log(Girth) + log(Height), family = Gamma(link = "log"), data = timber_data) 
summary(model_gamma_full)$deviance
```

```{r}
# =====================================================================
# Part G
# Approximate F-test comparing model from part E to part D
# The anova() function with test="F" automatically uses the Pearson 
# estimate of dispersion from the larger model
# =====================================================================
anova(model_gamma_null, model_gamma_girth, test = "F")
```

```{r}
# =====================================================================
# Part H
# Approximate F-test comparing model from part F to part E
# Using Pearson estimate of dispersion from the larger model
# =====================================================================
anova(model_gamma_girth, model_gamma_full, test = "F")
```

---

```{r}
#| message: false
#| warning: false
library(lme4)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

source("Recitation8.R")
```

```{r}
# =====================================================================
# Part A
# Fit random intercepts and random slopes model: 
# Effect of ses.dev on mathach varies between schools
# =====================================================================
model_2a <- lmer(mathach ~ ses.dev + (ses.dev | school), data = df)

# Report the variance components
summary(model_2a)
```

```{r}
VarCorr(model_2a)
```


```{r}
# =====================================================================
# Part B
# Include contextual variables mean.ses and sector as fixed effects
# =====================================================================
model_2b <- lmer(mathach ~ ses.dev + mean.ses + sector + (ses.dev | school), data = df)

summary(model_2b)
```

```{r}
# =====================================================================
# Part C
# Keep ses.dev slope fixed (random-intercept only model)
# =====================================================================
model_2c <- lmer(mathach ~ ses.dev + mean.ses + sector + (1 | school), data = df)

summary(model_2c)
```

```{r}
fixef(model_2c)
```


```{r}
# =====================================================================
# Part D
# Compare Model 2c (reduced) with Model 2b (full) 
# using a Likelihood Ratio Test
# =====================================================================
anova(model_2c, model_2b)
```

```{r}
# =====================================================================
# Part E
# Identify the "best 5" schools based on their intercepts 
# (fixed intercept + random intercept)
# =====================================================================
# The coef() function automatically adds the fixed intercept and random 
# effects together for each group.
school_intercepts <- coef(model_2c)$school

# Extract just the intercepts and keep school IDs
school_intercepts_df <- data.frame(
  school = rownames(school_intercepts),
  Total_Intercept = school_intercepts$`(Intercept)`
)

# Sort descending to find the top 5
top_5_schools <- school_intercepts_df %>%
  arrange(desc(Total_Intercept)) %>%
  head(5)

print(top_5_schools)
```

```{r}
# =====================================================================
# Part F
# Visualize random intercept distribution by sector with a boxplot
# =====================================================================
# First, we need to map the school sector back to our intercepts dataset
school_sectors <- df %>%
  select(school, sector) %>%
  distinct()

# Merge the total intercepts with the sector information
plot_data <- merge(school_intercepts_df, school_sectors, by = "school")

# Create the side-by-side boxplot
ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = sector, y = Total_Intercept, fill = sector)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of School Intercepts by Sector",
    x = "School Sector",
    y = "Estimated School Intercept (Fixed + Random)"
  ) +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
```
```{r}
# =====================================================================
# Problem 4 Setup
# Define global parameters and number of replications
# =====================================================================
R <- 100000       # Number of simulations
beta0 <- 10       # True intercept
beta1 <- 1        # True slope
n_vals <- c(25, 50, 100)
```

```{r}
# =====================================================================
# Part a) Data Generation & Average x
# Set seed, simulate x values once, and report averages
# =====================================================================
set.seed(549)

# Generate and store x values in a list so they remain fixed
x_list <- list()
for (n in n_vals) {
  x_list[[as.character(n)]] <- rnorm(n, mean = 0, sd = 1)
}

# Calculate and print the average of x for each sample size
x_averages <- sapply(x_list, mean)
print("Average x values for n = 25, 50, 100:")
print(x_averages)
```

```{r}
# =====================================================================
# Part b & c) Baseline Simulation (n=25, gamma=0)
# =====================================================================
n <- 25
gamma <- 0
x <- x_list[[as.character(n)]]

# 1. Calculate standard deviations (sqrt of variance)
sd_eps <- sqrt(exp(gamma * x))

# 2. Simulate errors (100,000 columns, 25 rows)
eps_mat <- matrix(rnorm(n * R, mean = 0, sd = sd_eps), nrow = n, ncol = R)

# 3. Compute responses Y
y_mat <- beta0 + beta1 * x + eps_mat

# 4. Fast Matrix OLS: beta_hat = (X'X)^(-1) X' Y
X_mat <- cbind(1, x)
H <- solve(t(X_mat) %*% X_mat) %*% t(X_mat)  # Projection matrix
beta_hats <- H %*% y_mat                     # Computes 100,000 regressions instantly

# Calculate and print averages
avg_beta0 <- mean(beta_hats[1, ])
avg_beta1 <- mean(beta_hats[2, ])

cat("Baseline Simulation (n=25, gamma=0)\n")
cat("Average Beta_0:", avg_beta0, "(True = 10)\n")
cat("Average Beta_1:", avg_beta1, "(True = 1)\n")
```

```{r}
# =====================================================================
# Part d) Expanded Heteroskedasticity Simulation
# Loop through all combinations of gamma and n
# =====================================================================
gamma_vals <- c(0, 1, 2)
results_het <- expand.grid(n = n_vals, gamma = gamma_vals)
results_het$avg_beta0 <- NA
results_het$avg_beta1 <- NA

for (i in 1:nrow(results_het)) {
  n <- results_het$n[i]
  g <- results_het$gamma[i]
  x <- x_list[[as.character(n)]]
  
  sd_eps <- sqrt(exp(g * x))
  eps_mat <- matrix(rnorm(n * R, mean = 0, sd = sd_eps), nrow = n, ncol = R)
  y_mat <- beta0 + beta1 * x + eps_mat
  
  X_mat <- cbind(1, x)
  H <- solve(t(X_mat) %*% X_mat) %*% t(X_mat)
  beta_hats <- H %*% y_mat
  
  results_het$avg_beta0[i] <- mean(beta_hats[1, ])
  results_het$avg_beta1[i] <- mean(beta_hats[2, ])
}

print("Heteroskedasticity Simulation Results:")
print(results_het)
```

```{r}
# =====================================================================
# Part e) Autoregression Model Simulation
# Loop through all combinations of rho and n
# =====================================================================
rho_vals <- c(0, 0.5, 0.9)
results_ar <- expand.grid(n = n_vals, rho = rho_vals)
results_ar$avg_beta0 <- NA
results_ar$avg_beta1 <- NA

for (i in 1:nrow(results_ar)) {
  n <- results_ar$n[i]
  rho <- results_ar$rho[i]
  x <- x_list[[as.character(n)]]
  
  # Initialize error matrix
  eps_mat <- matrix(0, nrow = n, ncol = R)
  eps_mat[1, ] <- 3  # Set epsilon_1 = 3 for all 100,000 paths
  
  # Generate standard normal u_i
  u_mat <- matrix(rnorm((n - 1) * R, mean = 0, sd = 1), nrow = n - 1, ncol = R)
  
  # Build the autoregressive series iteratively
  for (j in 2:n) {
    eps_mat[j, ] <- rho * eps_mat[j - 1, ] + u_mat[j - 1, ]
  }
  
  y_mat <- beta0 + beta1 * x + eps_mat
  
  X_mat <- cbind(1, x)
  H <- solve(t(X_mat) %*% X_mat) %*% t(X_mat)
  beta_hats <- H %*% y_mat
  
  results_ar$avg_beta0[i] <- mean(beta_hats[1, ])
  results_ar$avg_beta1[i] <- mean(beta_hats[2, ])
}

print("Autoregression Simulation Results:")
print(results_ar)
```
